title: Button
description: Displays a button or a component that looks like a button.

manual.title: Install dependencies
manual.description: Install the required dependencies for the button component.

manual.title: Add utility function
manual.description: Create a cn utility function if you don't have one already.

manual.title: Copy the component code
manual.description: Create a new file and paste the button component code.

examples.title: Default
Secondary
examples.description: Secondary variant — subdued background for lower-emphasis actions.

examples.title: Outline
examples.description: Outline variant — bordered button with transparent background.

examples.title: Ghost
examples.description: Ghost variant — no background until hovered.

examples.title: Destructive
examples.description: Destructive variant — red-toned button for dangerous or irreversible actions.

examples.title: Link
examples.description: Link variant — appears as an inline text link with underline on hover.

examples.title: Extra Small
Small
examples.description: size="sm" — small button for tight layouts.

examples.title: Large
examples.description: size="lg" — large button for prominent calls to action.

examples.title: With Icon
examples.description: Buttons with icons for enhanced visual communication.

examples.title: Button Group
examples.description:
To create a button group, use the ButtonGroup component.
 See the Button Group
 documentation for more details.
examples.code:
import * as React from "react";
import {
  ArchiveIcon,
  ArrowLeftIcon,
  CalendarPlusIcon,
  ClockIcon,
  ListFilterIcon,
  MailCheckIcon,
  MoreHorizontalIcon,
  TagIcon,
  Trash2Icon,
} from "lucide-react";

import { Button } from "@shery-ui/components";
import { ButtonGroup } from "@shery-ui/components";
import {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
} from "@shery-ui/components";

export default function ButtonGroupDemo({ customClassName }) {
  return (
    <ButtonGroup>
      <ButtonGroup className="hidden sm:flex">
        <Button variant="outline" size="icon" aria-label="Go Back">
          <ArrowLeftIcon />
        </Button>
      </ButtonGroup>
      <ButtonGroup>
        <Button variant="outline">Archive</Button>
        <Button variant="outline">Report</Button>
      </ButtonGroup>
      <ButtonGroup>
        <Button variant="outline">Snooze</Button>
        <DropdownMenu>
          <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
            <Button variant="outline" className={customClassName}>
              <MoreHorizontalIcon />
            </Button>
          </DropdownMenuTrigger>
          <DropdownMenuContent>
            <DropdownMenuItem className="flex gap-2">
              <MailCheckIcon />
              Mark as Read
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem className="flex gap-2">
              <ArchiveIcon />
              Archive
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuSeparator />
            <DropdownMenuItem className="flex gap-2">
              <ClockIcon />
              Snooze
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem className="flex gap-2">
              <CalendarPlusIcon />
              Add to Calendar
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem className="flex gap-2">
              <CalendarPlusIcon />
              Add to Calendar
            </DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem className="flex gap-2">
              <ListFilterIcon />
              Add to List
            </DropdownMenuItem>
          </DropdownMenuContent>
        </DropdownMenu>
      </ButtonGroup>
    </ButtonGroup>
  );
}

examples.title: As Child
examples.description: You can use the asChild prop on <Button /> to make another component look like a button. Here's an example of a link that looks like a button.
